_meta.signaturePurviewNote: signaturePurviewId is the Purview id on the character sheet (must exist in purviews.json). Boons list which Purview(s) gate them via `purview`, `purviews`, or `purviewAnyOf` in boons.json.
_meta.patronAssetSkills: At bundle load, each `deities` / `titans` row gains `assetSkills` copied from its pantheon if missing; `patronAssetSkillOverrides.json` then applies Pandora’s Box (or other) per-patron corrections (`pantheonId:patronId`). The chargen UI uses the selected parent’s `assetSkills` for Society Path (Origin pp. 96–97).

aesir.name: Æsir — Norse
aesir.description: Gods of the Norse sphere—fate, storms, and the long winter.
aesir.mechanicalEffects: Society Path grants these two Asset Skills plus one more Skill of your choice (Origin p. 97).
aesir.source: Scion_Origin_(Revised_Download).pdf, Character Creation pantheon list p. 95; Appendix 2 pp. 170–171

manitou.name: Manitou — Algonquian
manitou.description: Spirits and culture heroes tied to lakes, forests, and Skyworld stories.
manitou.mechanicalEffects: Society Path Asset Skills plus one more Skill (Origin p. 97).

theoi.name: Theoi — Greco-Roman
theoi.description: Olympians and their rivals—politics, passion, and polis.
theoi.mechanicalEffects: Society Path Asset Skills plus one more Skill (Origin p. 97).

netjer.name: Netjer — Egyptian
netjer.description: The court of lives, death, and starlit deserts.
netjer.mechanicalEffects: Society Path Asset Skills plus one more Skill (Origin p. 97).

kami.name: Kami — Japanese
kami.description: Amatsukami and Kunitsukami—myriad kami of shrine and story.
kami.mechanicalEffects: Society Path Asset Skills plus one more Skill (Origin p. 95 list).

tuathaDeDanann.name: Tuatha Dé Danann — Irish
tuathaDeDanann.description: Gods of hills, brughs, and bargains struck in torchlight.
tuathaDeDanann.mechanicalEffects: Society Path Asset Skills plus one more Skill (Origin p. 97).

orisha.name: Òrìshà — Yorùbá
orisha.description: Orishas of river, forge, and drum—community and trial woven together.
orisha.mechanicalEffects: Society Path Asset Skills plus one more Skill (Origin p. 97).

loa.name: Loa — Haiti
loa.description: The lwa of Vodou—Gods forged in the Americas from Fon, Yorùbá, and many African lines, cousins to the Òrìshà (Mysteries of the World).
loa.mechanicalEffects: Society Path: two pantheon Asset Skills plus one more Skill (Origin p. 97). Many Loa mirror Òrìshà Mantles unless MotW specifies otherwise. Favored Callings for patrons in this chapter follow Mysteries of the World p. 33–36 — do not take Pandora’s Box Birthrights “Calling:” on Guide NPC stat blocks (e.g. Baron Samedi PB p. 144) as the parent’s three Callings; that line is the Guide’s Storypath Calling only.

deities.name: Legba, Who Opens the Way
deities.source: Mysteries_of_the_World_-_Scion_Companion_(Final_Download).pdf p. 33 — Mantle of Èshù Elègbará (Favored Callings match Scion: Hero Èshù).

deities.name: Baron Cimetière
deities.source: Mysteries_of_the_World_-_Scion_Companion_(Final_Download).pdf p. 34 — The Barons (Favored Callings: Judge, Guardian, Sage for Cimetière / La Croix).

deities.name: Baron La Croix
deities.source: Mysteries_of_the_World_-_Scion_Companion_(Final_Download).pdf p. 34 — The Barons (Favored Callings: Judge, Guardian, Sage for Cimetière / La Croix).

deities.name: Baron Samedi
deities.source: Mysteries_of_the_World_-_Scion_Companion_(Final_Download).pdf p. 34 — The Barons (Favored Callings: Judge, Lover, Trickster for Samedi). SCION_Pandoras_Box_(Revised_Download).pdf p. 144 lists “Calling: Trickster” on the Guide birthright write-up only, not the parent triple (Origin p. 98).

deities.name: Damballa, the Ancestor
deities.source: Mysteries_of_the_World_-_Scion_Companion_(Final_Download).pdf p. 34 — Damballa (Favored Callings: Creator, Leader, Sage).

deities.name: Ezili, Who Struggles and Loves
deities.source: Mysteries_of_the_World_-_Scion_Companion_(Final_Download).pdf pp. 34–35 — Ezili Dantò / Ezili Freda (combined Scion entry; confirm Favored Callings at table).

deities.name: Kalfu, Who Walks at Night
deities.source: Mysteries_of_the_World_-_Scion_Companion_(Final_Download).pdf p. 35 — Kalfu (Favored Callings: Liminal, Sage, Trickster).

deities.name: Lasyren, Who Sings in the Sea
deities.source: Mysteries_of_the_World_-_Scion_Companion_(Final_Download).pdf p. 36 — Mantle of Yemoja-Oboto (Favored Callings match Scion: Hero Yemoja).

deities.name: Ogou, Who Wields Iron
deities.source: Mysteries_of_the_World_-_Scion_Companion_(Final_Download).pdf p. 36 — Mantle of Ògún (Favored Callings match Scion: Hero Ògún).

deva.name: Devá — South Asia
deva.description: Vedic currents, storm thrones, and wheel-turning kings.
deva.mechanicalEffects: Society Path Asset Skills plus one more Skill (Origin p. 97).

shen.name: Shén — Chinese
shen.description: Celestial bureaucracy, rivers of ink, and monkey-king mischief.
shen.mechanicalEffects: Society Path Asset Skills plus one more Skill (Origin p. 97).

teotl.name: Teōtl — Aztec
teotl.description: Flower wars, thirsty suns, and altars that remember.
teotl.mechanicalEffects: Society Path Asset Skills plus one more Skill (Origin p. 97).

nemetondevos.name: Nemetondevos — Sacred Shrines (Gaul)
nemetondevos.description: Reincarnating Gaulish Gods tied to nemetons—sacred shrines bridging The World and their realms—rebuilding after Rome (Mysteries of the World).
nemetondevos.mechanicalEffects: Society Path: two pantheon Asset Skills plus one more Skill (Origin p. 97). Signature Purview Nemeton models shrine gates and mementos; confirm Boons and limits in MotW.

deities.name: Cernunnos, Horned God of the Wild
Nantosuelta, Goddess of Hearth and Hive
Nehalennia and Nodens, Twins of Voyages and Healing Journeys
Taranis, God of the Sky and the Storm
Sulis, Goddess of the Healing Springs of Bath

yazata.name: Yazata — Persia
yazata.description: Ashavans of Mazdayasna—masters of good thoughts, words, and deeds—standing against the Druj and daevas (Mysteries of the World).
yazata.mechanicalEffects: Society Path: two pantheon Asset Skills plus one more Skill (Origin p. 97). Signature Purview Asha is the Threefold Path made playable; confirm Mantles and Demigod+ options in MotW.

deities.name: Hvare-Khshaeta, Who Is the Radiant Sun

mythos.name: The Mythos — Lovecraftian
mythos.description: Extradimensional entities and their Scions (Scion: Masks of the Mythos)—alien patterns, optional Awareness, Mythos Fatebindings, and realities pressed thin.
mythos.mechanicalEffects: Mythos Path Skills default to Academics and Occult (MotM). Use the supplement for Awareness, Mythos Conditions, investigation chapter, Miskatonic Hollow, antagonists, and relics not modeled in this JSON.

anunna.name: Anunna — Mesopotamian
anunna.description: The great courts of Sumer, Akkad, and Babylon—tablets, ziggurats, and gods who remember being first.
anunna.mechanicalEffects: Society Path: two pantheon Asset Skills plus one more Skill (Origin p. 97).
anunna.source: SCION_Pandoras_Box_(Revised_Download).pdf — Anunna pantheon; patron lists confirm against your PDFs.

bogovi.name: Bogovi — Slavic
bogovi.description: Forest law, storm kings, and the old pacts that keep the world turning under Slavic skies.
bogovi.mechanicalEffects: Society Path: two pantheon Asset Skills plus one more Skill (Origin p. 97).
bogovi.source: SCION_Pandoras_Box_(Revised_Download).pdf — Bogovi pantheon; confirm patrons at the table.

apu.name: Apu — Andean
apu.description: Apus of peak and storm—ancestors on the heights, lightning on the cordillera, and cities older than their stones remember.
apu.mechanicalEffects: Society Path: two pantheon Asset Skills plus one more Skill (Origin p. 97).

atua.name: Atua — Polynesian
atua.description: Sky, sea, and lineage woven together—atua who walk as storm, shark, and the obligations between cousins.
atua.mechanicalEffects: Society Path: two pantheon Asset Skills plus one more Skill (Origin p. 97).

balahala.name: Balahala — Philippine
balahala.description: Island spirits, ancestor bargains, and the bright edge between jungle and sea.
balahala.mechanicalEffects: Society Path: two pantheon Asset Skills plus one more Skill (Origin p. 97).

ilhm.name: Ilhm — Persian
ilhm.description: Feast-law, fire-temples, and guests bound by salt—Ilhm remembers the table as altar.
ilhm.mechanicalEffects: Society Path: two pantheon Asset Skills plus one more Skill (Origin p. 97).
ilhm.source: SCION_Pandoras_Box_(Revised_Download).pdf — Ilhm pantheon (distinct from Yazata in published chapters).

kuh.name: K’uh — Maya
kuh.description: Calendar priests, storm serpents, and the old cities whose stones still count days.
kuh.mechanicalEffects: Society Path: two pantheon Asset Skills plus one more Skill (Origin p. 97).

tengri.name: Tengri — Turko-Mongol
tengri.description: Eternal blue sky, steppe law, and shamans who bargain where soul meets storm.
tengri.mechanicalEffects: Society Path: two pantheon Asset Skills plus one more Skill (Origin p. 97).

palas.name: Pālas — Tibetan
palas.description: Vows, mandalas, and wrathful compassion worn like armor—Pālas walk the ridge between miracle and rule.
palas.mechanicalEffects: Society Path: two pantheon Asset Skills plus one more Skill (Origin p. 97).

zem.name: Zem — Taíno
zem.description: Caribbean atabeys and yucahuas—storm, cassava, and spirits who remember the islands before the maps.
zem.mechanicalEffects: Society Path: two pantheon Asset Skills plus one more Skill (Origin p. 97).
